# QC Genie

QC Genie is a web platform for pre-publish video quality control.

Primary audience: YouTube creators, video editors, studios, agencies, and agentic workflows that prepare digital media for publication.

Core workflow:
- Paste a YouTube URL, upload a video, or create a job through the API.
- QC Genie scans the full timeline for frozen frames, audio garble, caption and safe-area issues, aspect-ratio problems, and transcript-grounded review notes.
- Reports include verdict, timestamps, evidence, editor handoff notes, and export-ready artifacts.
- External full-video gate results can be imported with POST /v1/qc/jobs/{job_id}/gate-verdict after an agent runs scripts/qc-engine/run_gate.py.

Pricing:
- Creator: planned lightweight plan for Shorts and personal channels.
- Studio: $49/month for 2,000 QC minutes.
- Agency: planned team plan for workspaces, webhooks, and editor exports.

Agent integration:
- REST API job lifecycle starts at POST /v1/qc/jobs.
- OpenAPI: https://qcgenie-api.onrender.com/openapi.json
- Hosted API base: https://qcgenie-api.onrender.com/v1
- MCP wrapper package lives in the repository under mcp-server.
- MCP tools: qc_run_video, qc_get_job, qc_get_report, qc_get_events, qc_get_artifacts, qc_get_marker_csv, qc_submit_gate_verdict, qc_list_recent_jobs, qc_create_upload_url.
- API keys are workspace-scoped and should be stored as connector secrets.
- Agents should send idempotency_key on retries; repeated keys return the existing QC job instead of creating another run.
- Webhook endpoints can receive job.completed and job.failed events with X-QCGenie-Signature metadata.
- Completed jobs enqueue signed webhook delivery records; delivery logs are available at GET /v1/webhooks/deliveries, manual retries at POST /v1/webhooks/deliveries/{delivery_id}/retry, and due pending delivery drains at POST /v1/webhooks/deliveries/drain.
- Current v0 API persists jobs, uploads, webhook endpoints, webhook delivery previews, and usage entries to a server-side JSON store; production Supabase persistence is still planned.
- Report reads append rounded-minute usage entries visible through GET /v1/usage.
- Creating a QC job currently runs deterministic v0 processing immediately and stores lifecycle events, one caption-safe-area warning, and JSON/marker-export artifact records.
- Marker exports are available as CSV at GET /v1/qc/jobs/{job_id}/artifacts/markers for editor handoff.

Trust rule:
- Hard fails come from automated quality checks or explicit evidence.
- Advisory review notes cannot create invented hard-fails.
